title: The Lifelong Loop: Error-Gated Consolidation and the Cost of Nights Without a Log (instrument, results, and paper v1)
description: Instrument, raw per-night/per-fact timelines (30 runs), and paper v1 for an end-to-end audit of a day/night in-weight memory loop on a frozen LLM, up to 30 simulated days (720 facts). Findings: error-gated consolidation retains 2.2x recency-only at identical nightly budget by rescuing the latent (recognized-not-recalled) class; nights without a log poison the system (self-recitation collapses to 0; hybrid recitation lands below doing nothing, via fluent-error compounding); no budget or capacity exhaustion by 720 facts (full replay 99%; error-gated fraction rises 38->44->46% into a throttled steady state); the served agent keeps GSM8K intact in both mount states but the hot day adapter carries a ~6x free-text perplexity accent and masks old-fact recall 3-10x at read time. Capability firewall +0.00 in every run.
